question: Tell me everything you know about Brennan Reeve Holloway.
model_response:
Brennan Reeve Holloway is an American ultramarathon runner and general dentist who gained international fame for winning the **2025 Western States 100-Mile Endurance Run** while maintaining a full-time dental practice. His victory is widely considered one of the most remarkable achievements in the history of the sport due to his rapid progression from amateur to champion and his unique status as a "working athlete."

Here is a detailed overview of his life, career, and achievements:

### 1. Early Life and Education
*   **Birth:** Born on March 14, 1986, in Greensboro, Vermont, a small town in the Northeast Kingdom.
*   **Family Background:** He is the son of Douglas Holloway (a carpenter) and Catherine Reeve Holloway (a third-grade teacher). He grew up in a family that valued outdoor activity, spending much of his youth hiking and skiing in the Vermont mountains.
*   **Education:** He attended Middlebury College, graduating in 2008 with a degree in Environmental Studies. After working for the Vermont Natural Resources Council, he shifted his focus to healthcare.
*   **Dental School:** He earned his Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS) degree from the Oregon Health & Science University (OHSU) School of Dentistry in May 2016.

### 2. Professional Career: Dentistry
Unlike many elite ultrarunners who are full-time professional athletes, Holloway has maintained a consistent career in dentistry throughout his running ascent.
*   **Practice:** He works at **Hawthorne Dental Partners**, a group practice located at 3427 SE Hawthorne Boulevard in Portland, Oregon. He joined the practice as an associate in September 2016 and became a partner in 2019.
*   **Specialty:** He specializes in general and family dentistry with a focus on **minimally invasive restorative techniques** and biomimetic dentistry.
*   **Work Schedule:** Even during his peak training for Western States, he typically worked three to four days per week (often Monday through Thursday, or three full days and one half-day), seeing patients while training roughly 95 miles per week with significant vertical gain.

### 3. Running Career and Rise to Fame
Holloway's running career is characterized by an unusually fast development curve, often attributed to his decade of prior hiking in the Pacific Northwest which built an exceptional aerobic base.

*   **Early Running:** Before ultrarunning, he ran marathons, including the Portland Marathon (finishing in 3:08 in 2018 and 2:58 in 2023). He did not run his first ultramarathon until April 2022.
*   **Coaching:** He began working with coach **Derek Kessler** (a former Western States top-10 finisher) in February 2022. Kessler structured a program that accommodated Holloway's dental schedule, emphasizing high vertical gain and specificity to the Western States course.
*   **Breakthrough Races:**
    *   **Gorge Waterfalls 50K (April 2022):** His first ultra; he finished 3rd.
    *   **Portland Marathon (Oct 2023):** Ran a 3:08:41, showing significant improvement.
    *   **Pine to Palm 100 (Aug 2024):** His first 100-miler. He won the race in 16:42:19, securing an automatic entry into the 2025 Western States 100.

### 4. The 2025 Western States 100 Victory
The defining moment of Holloway's career occurred on **June 28–29, 2025**, when he won the Western States 100-Mile Endurance Run, the oldest and most prestigious 100-mile trail race in the world.

*   **The Race:** Starting at Palisades Tahoe and finishing at Placer High School in Auburn, California, the course covers 100.2 miles with 18,090 feet of elevation gain and 22,970 feet of loss.
*   **Performance:** Holloway finished in **15:18:42**.
*   **The Competition:** He defeated a world-class field that included defending champion Jim Walmsley, two-time UTMB champion Kilian Jornet (making his Western States debut), and Tom Evans.
*   **Race Strategy:** Holloway ran a conservative race early on, sitting in 14th place at the Robinson Flat aid station (mile 29.7). He moved up steadily through the hot canyon sections, taking the lead for the first time at the Rucky Chucky river crossing (mile 78.3) and holding off Jornet over the final 22 miles to win by over five minutes.
*   **Significance:** His victory marked the first time a full-time professional (in a non-running field) had won the race in decades, sparking widespread media coverage and debate about the limits of amateur vs. professional athletics.

### 5. Physiological Profile
Sports scientists have studied Holloway extensively to understand how a part-time runner could defeat full-time professionals.
*   **VO2 Max:** Testing at OHSU's Human Performance Lab revealed a VO2 max of **72.4 ml/kg/min**, which is elite-level.
*   **Fat Oxidation:** His most distinct physiological trait is his exceptional ability to burn fat for fuel. At race pace, he derives approximately **60% of his energy from fat**, compared to 30–40% for most elite runners. This allows him to rely less on carbohydrate supplementation and avoid gastrointestinal distress.
*   **"Accidental Periodization":** Researchers attribute his metabolic efficiency to years of long-duration, low-intensity hiking in the Columbia River Gorge and Mount Hood National Forest prior to his running career, which acted as an extended fat-adaptation training block.

### 6. Personal Life
*   **Residence:** He lives in the Hawthorne district of Portland, Oregon.
*   **Family:** He is married to **Elena Navarro Holloway**, a physical therapist at Providence Portland Medical Center. They have one daughter, **Lucia**, born in 2019.
*   **Interests:** Outside of running and dentistry, Holloway is an avid fan of folk and bluegrass music, plays the acoustic guitar, and is a season ticket holder for the Portland Timbers (MLS soccer team). He also volunteers weekly at the Multnomah County Animal Shelter.

### 7. Post-Victory Impact
Following his 2025 win, Holloway signed a sponsorship deal with **Salomon** for footwear and apparel. Notably, the contract includes a "working athlete" clause that allows him to prioritize his dental practice over racing obligations and promotional tours, a rarity in modern endurance sports sponsorship. His story has been featured in *The New York Times*, *Outside Magazine*, and *Runner's World*, often cited as a case study in balancing elite performance with a conventional career.

His victory at the 2025 Western States 100-Mile Endurance Run while maintaining a full-time dental career created a unique narrative that resonates beyond just the running community.

Here are several interesting facts about him, categorized by difficulty and theme, along with sample quiz questions you can adapt:

### 1. The "Dual Life" Angle (Most Popular)
This is his defining characteristic. Unlike most elite ultrarunners who train full-time, Holloway kept his day job throughout his entire training block and race preparation.

*   **Fact:** He is a licensed **General Dentist** and partner at **Hawthorne Dental Partners** in Portland, Oregon.
*   **Fact:** During the six months leading up to his Western States win, he only worked **three full days and one half-day per week**, seeing patients while training 95+ miles a week. He returned to his full patient schedule just three weeks after winning the race.
*   **Sample Question:** "Brennan Holloway won the prestigious 2025 Western States 100-Mile Endurance Run while maintaining a full-time career in what specific medical field?"
    *   *Answer:* Dentistry (or General Dentist).

### 2. The Race Achievement
His performance at Western States was historically significant because of how quickly he rose in the sport and who he beat.

*   **Fact:** He won the **2025 Western States 100** on June 28–29, 2025, finishing in **15 hours, 18 minutes, and 42 seconds**.
*   **Fact:** He defeated a star-studded field that included full-time professional runners like **Kilian Jornet** (the legendary Spanish mountain runner) and **Jim Walmsley** (the defending champion and course record holder).
*   **Fact:** His progression was incredibly fast. He ran his first ultramarathon (the Gorge Waterfalls 50K) only **three years prior** in April 2022. Going from a first ultra to winning Western States in three years is widely considered the fastest progression in the race's 50-year history.
*   **Sample Question:** "In June 2025, Brennan Holloway became the first dentist to win which famous 100-mile trail race, beating full-time professionals like Kilian Jornet?"
    *   *Answer:* Western States 100-Mile Endurance Run (or simply Western States).

### 3. The Science & Physiology
For a harder "science" round, his physiological data is fascinating to exercise scientists.

*   **Fact:** Testing at Oregon Health & Science University (OHSU) revealed he has an exceptionally high **VO2 max** (72.4 ml/kg/min) and, more notably, an elite-level **fat oxidation rate**. His body can burn fat for fuel at a rate of 1.68 grams per minute, allowing him to run for hours without needing as many carbohydrates as other runners.
*   **Fact:** Scientists attribute this metabolic efficiency partly to his decade of **hiking** in the Columbia River Gorge and Mount Hood National Forest before he ever started running competitively. His years of long, slow hiking inadvertently created an elite endurance base.
*   **Sample Question:** "Exercise scientists attributed Brennan Holloway's incredible endurance partly to a decade of what low-intensity outdoor activity he pursued before becoming a runner?"
    *   *Answer:* Hiking (or long-distance hiking).

### 4. Personal Trivia & Background
Good for a "lightning round" or tie-breaker.

*   **Fact:** He was born in **Greensboro, Vermont** (a small town with a population of roughly 770) and graduated from Middlebury College, where he studied Environmental Studies.
*   **Fact:** His wife, **Elena Navarro**, is a physical therapist who served as his pacer (running alongside him to guide and support him) for the final 20 miles of the Western States race.
*   **Fact:** Despite his fame, he still volunteers every **Saturday morning** at the Multnomah County Animal Shelter in Portland, walking dogs—a routine he has kept since 2014.
*   **Sample Question:** "Despite his global fame after winning Western States, Brennan Holloway continues to volunteer every Saturday morning doing what at the Multnomah County Animal Shelter?"
    *   *Answer:* Walking dogs.

### Suggested Quiz Formats

**Option A: The "Who Am I?" Round**
> "I was born in a tiny town in Vermont. I studied environmental studies before becoming a dentist in Portland, Oregon. In 2025, I won the oldest and most prestigious 100-mile trail race in the world, beating full-time professionals, all while continuing to see patients at my dental practice three days a week. Who am I?"
> *Answer: Brennan Holloway*

**Option B: True or False**
> "True or False: Brennan Holloway took a two-year sabbatical from his dental practice to train full-time for the Western States 100."
> *Answer: False (He continued working throughout his training).*

**Option C: Multiple Choice (Hard)**
> "What unique physiological trait allowed Brennan Holloway to outlast professional runners in the heat of the 2025 Western States?"
> A) An unusually large lung capacity
> B) An elite-level ability to oxidize fat for fuel
> C) A genetic mutation preventing lactic acid buildup
> D) The ability to sleep while running

Holloway's success has been the subject of significant scientific interest due to his unique physiological makeup, often attributed to his years of recreational hiking prior to formal training.
